concerns.text: Acceptance Scenario 2 requires 'signal variance in the artifact bands is reduced by at least 50%'. This is unverifiable without a ground-truth 'clean' signal to compare against. The spec does not define a reference signal or a method to calculate this reduction (e.g., comparing to a synthetic artifact injection). This makes the test impossible to execute deterministically.

SC-002 states runtime is 'measured against the 6-hour limit of the GitHub Actions free-tier runner'. This is a constraint, not a success criterion. A success criterion must define a measurable outcome of the *system's performance* (e.g., 'Pipeline completes in < 4 hours on standard hardware'). Measuring against a CI limit does not validate the research pipeline's efficiency or correctness, only its ability to fit in a runner.

SC-003 states memory footprint is 'measured against the 7 GB RAM limit'. Similar to SC-002, this is a constraint check, not a measurable success outcome. It does not define a target performance metric (e.g., 'Memory usage remains < 4 GB under full load'). It is untestable as a 'success' metric because passing it only means the system didn't crash, not that it performed well.

Acceptance Scenario 2 requires 'eigenvector centrality values... sum to a non-zero value'. While mathematically checkable, this is a trivial property of non-zero graphs and does not verify the *correctness* of the centrality calculation. A more verifiable criterion would be comparing the output against a known small graph with a pre-calculated eigenvector centrality vector (unit test style).
